name: Gorgophone
shortDescription: Gorgophone was a princess of Argos and a significant figure in Greek mythology, known as the 'slayer of Gorgon' due to her descendants' connection to Perseus.
description: Gorgophone, whose name means 'Gorgon Slayer' or 'Gorgon Killer,' was a princess of Argos and a pivotal figure in the lineage of Perseus. She was the daughter of Perseus and Andromeda. Gorgophone holds a unique place in Greek mythology as the first mortal woman to remarry after the death of her first husband. Her first marriage was to Perieres, king of Messenia, with whom she had several sons, including Aphareus and Leucippus. After Perieres' death, she married Oebalus of Sparta, further solidifying her connection to prominent royal families. This act of remarriage was considered unusual in ancient Greece and highlights her strong will and independent nature. Her descendants played significant roles in various myths and heroic sagas, linking her indirectly to the slaying of Medusa through her father, Perseus. Although Gorgophone herself did not directly participate in the Gorgon's slaying, her name and the legacy of her father firmly associate her with that iconic event. Her story emphasizes the importance of lineage and the impact of individuals on the course of mythological history. She represents a break from traditional expectations of women and a connection to the heroic legacy of Perseus.

sources.title: The History of the Peloponnesian War
sources.author: Thucydides
